safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_live 2026-06-05 chart — green demand zone at $71.83 (text mention said ~$71); the plan is sweep the low, reclaim, then long/spot ON confirmation only. Note: the chart also marks $78 as a first 'SD-gap rebalance minimum' before this deeper demand.

targets.basis: near-term prior structure / recent range high on chart (estimated)
zero_live chart — '0.5 gap' / underside retest of value area at $100.05

safest_plan.stop_basis: Long is invalid if price loses the $71.83 demand zone without reclaiming structure; next major support is the '12M + multi HTF level' at roughly $56–60 on the chart (estimated stop placement below demand).

stance_by_source.summary: Expects price to drop first — rebalancing a gap down to about $78, ideally sweeping the deeper demand near $71.83 — then wants to buy only if price reclaims structure on confirmation; warns the wider market (S&P 500 at record highs) makes this risky.

conflicts: The chart shows a first downside target of $78 (a gap that needs filling) that the extracted text left out; the text only named the deeper $71.83 demand zone. Both are valid — $78 is the shallow target, $71.83 the deeper buy zone.
Same source is near-term bearish (waiting for a move lower) but sets up a longer-term long off the demand zone — so there is no trade to take now, only a level to watch.
plain_english: Only one source covered Netflix, and it is telling you to wait, not buy. The view is that the price should fall first — down to around $78, and ideally as low as about $72 — before any buying opportunity appears. Even then, you would only buy if the price drops to that zone and then turns back up to prove itself, and there is added caution because the overall stock market is at record highs and could pull everything down. For now: do nothing, set an alert near $72, and only consider a long if it dips there and shows a clear bounce.
